title: Classifiers: Basics
description: Match the right classifier to common nouns — essential for building noun phrases
intro: In Vietnamese, you can't just say 'a coffee' — you need a classifier word: 'một LY cà phê'. Each noun has a specific classifier based on its shape, size, or category.

questions.prompt: What classifier do you use for a cup of coffee? 'một ___ cà phê'
questions.explanation: 'ly' is used for glasses/cups of liquid: ly cà phê, ly nước, ly bia

questions.prompt: What classifier do you use for a bottle of water? 'một ___ nước'
questions.explanation: 'chai' is for bottles: chai nước, chai bia, chai rượu

questions.prompt: What classifier do you use for animals? 'một ___ chó (dog)'
questions.explanation: 'con' is the universal classifier for animals: con chó, con mèo, con cá

questions.prompt: What is the most general classifier in Vietnamese?
questions.explanation: 'cái' is the default classifier for inanimate objects. When in doubt, 'cái' often works
'bát' (Northern) or 'tô' (Southern) is for bowls of food: bát phở, bát cơm

questions.prompt: Một ___ bánh mì (a piece of bread/sandwich)
questions.explanation: 'ổ' is for loaves of bread: ổ bánh mì
'cái bàn' — 'cái' is used for common objects like tables, chairs, spoons

questions.prompt: 'Con' is used for animals AND for what else?
questions.explanation: 'con sông' (river), 'con đường' (road) — things that are long and winding, like animals
'lon' is for cans: lon bia, lon nước ngọt

questions.prompt: Complete the order: 'Cho tôi một ___ cà phê sữa đá'
questions.explanation: You order drinks with 'ly': một ly cà phê = a cup of coffee
'con' for all animals: con mèo (cat), con chó (dog), con gà (chicken)
Tea is a drink, so use 'ly' (glass/cup): một ly trà = a cup of tea
'cái' for common inanimate objects: cái ghế (chair), cái bàn (table)

questions.prompt: Which classifier would you use for 'nước' (water) in a bottle?
questions.explanation: chai nước = bottle of water. The classifier depends on the container, not just the liquid
Fish are animals → 'con': năm con cá

options: bát is formal, tô is casual
questions.explanation: Both mean 'bowl'. Northern speakers say 'bát', Southern say 'tô'
